title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Programs, Packages, and Evidence · Go Retrieval
summary: A small operations team needs a reproducible status tool, but its source, package boundary, formatter, build, runtime, and release identity are unclear. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reproducible first-program and toolchain evidence record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.
Choose and interpret go run, build, install, test, fmt, vet, doc, env, version, and help evidence for a bounded task.
Apply UTF-8 source, identifiers, keywords, tokens, semicolon insertion, comments, and formatting rules without relying on visual coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

review case — A review status command is tested with Go 1.26.5 for linux/amd64 and the browser Yaegi subset. Predict package initialization, output, artifact identity, and which go fmt, test, vet, build, env, doc, or version evidence is still missing before release 4.

stimulus.title: First Evidence Loop case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review status command is tested with Go 1.26.5 for linux/amd64 and the browser Yaegi subset. Predict package initialization, output, artifact identity, and which go fmt, test, vet, build, env, doc, or version evidence is still missing before release 5.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One successful hello-world run demonstrates programming mastery.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict output, run the smallest program, inspect diagnostics, change one input, and preserve a regression assertion. Artifact under review: a reproducible first-program and toolchain evidence record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies First Evidence Loop, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient First Evidence Loop happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
